Est. 2001 Pittsburgh's Korean Kitchen & Sushi Bar

Soulful
Korean cooking,
정성껏
with care.

Twenty-five years of slow-simmered stews, charcoal-kissed bulgogi, and hand-cut sashimi at 110 Smithfield — a quiet downtown room where Kim family recipes meet the day's catch.

House Specialties 집 요리

Dishes we've perfected
over 이십오 년 — twenty-five years.

Full Menu →
불고기
01

Beef Bul Go Gi

Sliced tender prime beef marinated in homemade Korean BBQ sauce, grilled to perfection.

갈비구이
02

Gal Bi Gui

Tender beef short ribs marinated in a rich Korean BBQ sauce, grilled to a smoky, caramelized finish.

비빔밥
03

Bibimbap

A vibrant Korean rice bowl topped with seasoned vegetables, beef, and a fried egg. Served with house gochujang.

갈비탕
04

Galbi Tang

Beef short rib soup simmered with radish, onions, and glass noodles in a clear, deeply savory broth.

스시
05

Chef's Sushi Platter

Chef's selection, hand-cut daily. Bluefin, hamachi, uni, saba. Served with miso soup.

사시미
06

Sashimi

Today's catch, sliced to order. Served with wasabi, shiso, and pickled ginger.

FAMILY PORTRAIT · DROP IN
김 가족
THE KIM FAMILY · 2001–PRESENT

Our Story 우리 이야기

A family recipe,
two generations
in downtown Pittsburgh.

The Kim family opened their first kitchen in 2001 with a handwritten menu and a stone bibimbap bowl brought from Seoul. A quarter century later, the bowl is still on the pass — and the recipes haven't changed.

Read Our Story →

What Pittsburgh Is Saying

4.8 ★ across 200+ reviews.

★★★★★

"The galbi here is the best I've had outside of Seoul. A downtown Pittsburgh treasure."

— Michelle L. · Google
★★★★★

"Twenty-five years strong for a reason. The Kim family cooks with love."

— David R. · Google
★★★★★

"Sashimi is hand-cut and perfect. Banchan is generous and thoughtful."

— Priya S. · Yelp

Visit Us

110 Smithfield St.
Downtown.

A block from Market Square. Lunch service starts at 11:30. Dinner 4:30 to 9. Closed Mondays.
